input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan:

---

## .NET MAUI Dev Setup — Windows 11 (Android + Windows)

### 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download latest .NET SDK (8 or 9) from https://dot.net/download
# Or via winget:
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify:
dotnet --list-sdks
```

### 2. Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
dotnet workload install maui
# Verify installed workloads:
dotnet workload list
```

### 3. Install Visual Studio 2022 (recommended) or Build Tools

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community
# Then add required workload components via VS Installer, or CLI:
# (If using Build Tools instead, replace "Community" with "BuildTools")
```

Required VS workloads (via Installer UI or CLI):

```powershell
# CLI approach — modify existing install:
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --includeRecommended --quiet
```

### 4. Install OpenJDK (required for Android)

```powershell
# MAUI workload bundles Microsoft OpenJDK, but to install manually:
winget install Microsoft.OpenJDK.17
# Verify:
java -version
```

### 5. Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload installs Android SDK components, but you can manage them explicitly:

```powershell
# Discover where Android SDK was installed:
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdk)) {
    $androidSdk = "$env:ProgramFiles(x86)\Android\android-sdk"
}
Write-Host "Android SDK: $androidSdk"

# Add to PATH for current session:
$env:ANDROID_HOME = $androidSdk
$env:PATH += ";$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin;$androidSdk\platform-tools"

# List available platforms/build-tools dynamically:
sdkmanager --list | Select-String "build-tools|platforms;android-"

# Install required components (pick versions from output above):
sdkmanager "platform-tools" "platforms;android-34" "build-tools;34.0.0"

# Accept all licenses:
sdkmanager --licenses
```

### 6. Android Emulator (optional but recommended)

```powershell
sdkmanager "emulator" "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD:
avdmanager create avd -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-34;google_apis;x86_64" -d "pixel_5"

# Verify Hyper-V / WHPX is enabled (needed for emulator acceleration):
Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All |
    Select-Object FeatureName, State

# Enable if needed (requires reboot):
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All -NoRestart
```

### 7. Windows App SDK (for Windows target)

```powershell
# Usually included with VS workload, but to install standalone:
winget install Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK.1.6
# Or discover latest version:
winget search Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK
```

### 8. Enable Developer Mode (required for Windows target)

```powershell
# Check current state:
reg query "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock"

# Enable developer mode:
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

### 9. Create & Verify a Test Project

```powershell
# Create a new MAUI app:
dotnet new maui -n MauiTestApp
cd MauiTestApp

# Build for Windows:
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android:
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows:
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first):
emulator -avd Pixel_MAUI &
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

### 10. Discover Target Framework Versions Dynamically

```powershell
# See what TFMs your SDK supports:
dotnet --list-runtimes

# See available MAUI templates and their default TFMs:
dotnet new maui --dry-run -n Probe | Select-String "net\d"

# Check installed Android API levels:
sdkmanager --list_installed 2>$null | Select-String "platforms;android"

# Check installed Windows SDK versions:
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include" |
    Select-Object Name | Sort-Object Name -Descending
```

---

**Key version notes:** Replace `net9.0` with `net8.0` if you installed .NET 8. The Android API level (`android-34`) and Windows SDK version (`10.0.19041.0`) should match what you discovered in step 10. Run `dotnet workload update` periodically to stay current.
